subject: Configuration changes to make osdf ssl enabled
commit_message:
Configuration changes to make osdf ssl enabled

Add common_config.yaml file to enable SSL
Add the cert and key files
Modify the deployment to mount the cert files
Increment the docker image version
Securing HAS api with https
Modifying the health check protocol to https and credentials
Updating certs to add has hostnames

Change-Id: I860a0c436234b31d675ca0447fa92d511be0b140
Signed-off-by: vrvarma <vv8305@att.com>
Issue-ID: OPTFRA-293

Patch Set 6:

Not sure why the gitlab orange pipeline is failing.

"\tkubernetes/aai/templates/service.yaml", "\tkubernetes/aai/values.yaml", "Please move or remove them before you switch branches.", "Aborting", "-----------------------"]}


I was able to cherry-pick this configuration and was able to deploy it in sb-01.  and it is working fine.

Patch Set 6:

the AAI charts have transitioned to a submodule. is the Orange deployer cloning/initialising the submodules of OOM?

Patch Set 6:

> Patch Set 6:
> 
> the AAI charts have transitioned to a submodule. is the Orange deployer cloning/initialising the submodules of OOM?

Orange depleployed is including the submodules but there is an issue when changes are pushed into gerrit prior to the introduction of the submodules on dev side. Our gerrit review -d <gerrit_id> will systematically fail (there will be a git conflict).

Jessica from LF had a look. Her conclusion is "What devs need to do is to "git reset HEAD kubernetes/aai/" and proceed with "git commit" and push their change and it will be rebased into the latest tip. "

Hi Morgan, This commit is for the OSDF repo within the oof project, which is passing the health check (please see Basic OOF-SNIRO Health Check in the Gitlab Orange 2 run at 12:34 pm on patchiest 7). The homing health check is for the HAS within the OOF project, which should pass the health check once the following commit is merged (https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/86209/). Given that SNIRO health check is looking good, can we go ahead and merge this commit ?

Patch Set 8:

Hi Sylvain, This commit is for the OSDF repo within the oof project, which is passing the health check. The homing health check is for the HAS within the OOF project, which should pass the health check once the following commit is merged (https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/86209/). Similarly, https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/86672/ is for the CMSO and should be fixing the CMSO health check. Given that SNIRO health check is looking good, can we go ahead and merge this commit ?
